title: Patru întrebări pentru pesediști și nu doar pentru ei
summary: Cristian Preda critiques PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu's proposal for a new internal party consultation, arguing the questions PSD poses resemble those of an opposition party despite being in government. Preda proposes four alternative questions he considers more important for Romanian citizens, challenging PSD's nearly 14-year grip on governance and Grindeanu's controversial political record.
talking_points: PSD launched a new internal consultation before finishing the previous one announced in December, revealing organizational dysfunction
The party's proposed questions—whether to keep PM Bolojan and whether USR should remain in government—are opposition-style questions from a governing party
PSD's electoral base is a small minority: only 19% in presidential elections and 22% in legislative elections with 52% turnout
After nearly 14 years in government producing five prime ministers (Ponta, Grindeanu, Tudose, Dăncilă, Ciolacu), the author argues PSD should move to opposition
Grindeanu's previous tenure as PM was marked by OUG 13, which triggered the largest street protests in Romania's political history
author: Cristian Preda
